data.content:
### Run Playwright Tests in Headed Mode (C#)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-csharp.md

These commands allow running Playwright tests with a visible browser window, rather than the default headless mode. This is useful for visual debugging and observation of test execution. It involves setting an environment variable before invoking `dotnet test`.

```bash
HEADED=1 dotnet test
```

```batch
set HEADED=1
dotnet test
```

```powershell
$env:HEADED="1"
dotnet test
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Verbose API Logging with DEBUG Environment Variable

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/debug.md

Sets the DEBUG environment variable to 'pw:api' to enable detailed API logging for Playwright operations. Helps track all API calls made during test execution. Configuration varies by shell environment and programming language.

```bash
DEBUG=pw:api npx playwright test
```

```batch
set DEBUG=pw:api
npx playwright test
```

```powershell
$env:DEBUG="pw:api"
npx playwright test
```

```bash
DEBUG=pw:api mvn test
```

```batch
set DEBUG=pw:api
mvn test
```

```powershell
$env:DEBUG="pw:api"
mvn test
```

```bash
DEBUG=pw:api pytest -s
```

```batch
set DEBUG=pw:api
pytest -s
```

```powershell
$env:DEBUG="pw:api"
pytest -s
```

```bash
DEBUG=pw:api dotnet run
```

```batch
set DEBUG=pw:api
dotnet run
```

```powershell
$env:DEBUG="pw:api"
dotnet run
```

--------------------------------

### Control Headless Mode for Playwright Browser Launches

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-api/class-testoptions.md

Determines if the browser should run in headless mode (without a visible UI). This option overrides general `launchOptions` for headless settings.

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  use: {
    headless: false
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright tests in debug mode (C#, Java, Python)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/debug.md

Set the `PWDEBUG=1` environment variable to activate Playwright's debug mode for tests written in C#, Java, or Python. This configuration launches browsers in headed mode, removes default timeouts, and opens the Inspector for detailed debugging across different environments.

```bash
PWDEBUG=1 pytest -s
```

```batch
set PWDEBUG=1
pytest -s
```

```powershell
$env:PWDEBUG=1
pytest -s
```

```bash
PWDEBUG=1 dotnet test
```

```batch
set PWDEBUG=1
dotnet test
```

```powershell
$env:PWDEBUG=1
dotnet test
```

--------------------------------

### Configure New Chromium Headless Mode

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-csharp.md

Enables the new Chromium headless mode by specifying the 'chromium' channel in the run settings.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <Playwright>
    <BrowserName>chromium</BrowserName>
    <LaunchOptions>
      <Channel>chromium</Channel>
    </LaunchOptions>
  </Playwright>
</RunSettings>
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Playwright Browser with Headless Mode Disabled and SlowMo (Java)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/intro-java.md

This Java code shows how to launch a Playwright Firefox browser with specific options. It disables headless mode (`setHeadless(false)`) to make the browser UI visible, and sets a `slowMo` delay of 50 milliseconds (`setSlowMo(50)`) for slower, more observable execution of actions. This configuration is particularly useful during debugging and development.

```java
playwright.firefox().launch(new BrowserType.LaunchOptions().setHeadless(false).setSlowMo(50));
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Chromium Browser with New Headless Mode in Java

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-java.md

Demonstrates how to opt into the new Chromium headless mode using the 'chromium' channel. The new headless mode provides a more authentic Chrome browser experience compared to the old headless mode, making it more suitable for high-accuracy end-to-end testing and browser extension testing.

```java
Browser browser = playwright.chromium().launch(new BrowserType.LaunchOptions().setChannel("chromium"));
```

--------------------------------

### Run all Playwright tests in debug mode (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/debug.md

Execute all Playwright tests in JavaScript using the `--debug` flag to activate the Playwright Inspector. This command launches browsers in headed mode, disables default timeouts, and runs tests one by one for detailed debugging.

```bash
npx playwright test --debug
```

--------------------------------

### Run Tests with New Chromium Headless Mode via CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-csharp.md

Executes tests using the new Chromium headless mode by passing browser and channel configurations as command-line arguments.

```bash
dotnet test -- Playwright.BrowserName=chromium Playwright.LaunchOptions.Channel=chromium
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Browser in Headed Mode with Slow Motion for Debugging

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/debug.md

Launches browser in headed mode (visible UI) with optional slow motion delay between operations for debugging purposes. The slowMo option adds a delay in milliseconds between each operation to make test execution easier to follow visually.

```javascript
// Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit
await chromium.launch({ headless: false, slowMo: 100 });
```

```java
// Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit
chromium.launch(new BrowserType.LaunchOptions()
  .setHeadless(false)
  .setSlowMo(100));
```

```python
# Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit
await chromium.launch(headless=False, slow_mo=100)
```

```python
# Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit
chromium.launch(headless=False, slow_mo=100)
```

```csharp
// Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit
await using var browser = await playwright.Chromium.LaunchAsync(new()
{
    Headless = false,
    SlowMo = 100
});
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Tests with Default Configuration using npx

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-js.md

Executes all Playwright tests as configured in playwright.config file. Tests run in parallel and headless mode by default across all configured browsers, with results displayed in the terminal.

```bash
npx playwright test
```

--------------------------------

### Run Pytest with Chromium Channel for New Headless Mode

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-python.md

This command demonstrates how to execute Playwright tests using `pytest` with the new Chromium headless mode. By specifying `--browser-channel chromium`, users opt into the more authentic and reliable headless browser environment.

```bash
pytest test_login.py --browser-channel chromium
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Tests in UI Mode for Interactive Debugging

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-js.md

Launches Playwright UI Mode, providing an interactive developer experience to walk through each test step visually. UI Mode includes features like locator picker, watch mode, and step-by-step test execution visualization.

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Console Debug Mode for JavaScript Tests on Different Shells

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/debug.md

Shows how to run Playwright JavaScript tests with console debug mode enabled using the PWDEBUG environment variable. The commands differ based on the shell environment (bash, Windows batch, PowerShell). This enables the playwright object in browser developer tools for DOM inspection and debugging.

```bash
PWDEBUG=console npx playwright test
```

```batch
set PWDEBUG=console
npx playwright test
```

```powershell
$env:PWDEBUG="console"
npx playwright test
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Playwright browser in non-headless mode with slow motion (C#)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/library-csharp.md

This C# example shows how to launch a Firefox browser instance with the UI visible (`Headless = false`) and slow down execution by 50 milliseconds (`SlowMo = 50`). This is useful for debugging and observing automation steps.

```csharp
await using var browser = await playwright.Firefox.LaunchAsync(new()
{
    Headless = false,
    SlowMo = 50,
});
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Console Debug Mode for Java Tests with Source Directory Configuration

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/debug.md

Demonstrates running Playwright Java tests with console debug mode and source directory configuration. Requires setting both PWDEBUG and PLAYWRIGHT_JAVA_SRC environment variables. Note that source directories are separated by colon on macOS/Linux and semicolon on Windows. Commands vary by shell environment.

```bash
# Source directories in the list are separated by : on macos and linux and by ; on win.
PWDEBUG=console PLAYWRIGHT_JAVA_SRC=<java source dirs> mvn test
```

```batch
# Source directories in the list are separated by : on macos and linux and by ; on win.
set PLAYWRIGHT_JAVA_SRC=<java source dirs>
set PWDEBUG=console
mvn test
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Playwright Browser in Headed Mode with Slow Motion in Python

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/library-python.md

Configures Playwright Firefox browser to run in headed mode (visible UI) with slowed execution for debugging purposes. The headless=False parameter displays the browser window, while slow_mo=50 adds a 50ms delay between operations for easier visual debugging.

```python
firefox.launch(headless=False, slow_mo=50)
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Tests in Headed Browser CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command executes Playwright tests in browsers with a visible UI, as opposed to the default headless mode. Useful for visual debugging and observation.

```bash
# Run tests in headed browsers
npx playwright test --headed
```

--------------------------------

### Add programmatic breakpoints with `page.pause()` in Playwright

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/debug.md

Insert `page.pause()` into your test code to create a programmatic breakpoint, which halts test execution at that exact line when running in debug mode. This method is available across JavaScript, Java, Python, and C# Playwright APIs, allowing you to jump directly to specific points for inspection.

```javascript
await page.pause();
```

```java
page.pause();
```

```python
await page.pause()
```

```python
page.pause()
```

```csharp
await page.PauseAsync();
```

--------------------------------

### Configuring Playwright to use New Chromium Headless Mode (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-js.md

This configuration snippet shows how to set up Playwright to use the 'chromium' channel for its projects, which enables the new, more authentic Chromium headless mode. This is configured within `playwright.config.ts` and is useful for high-accuracy end-to-end testing and browser extension testing.

```js
import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  projects: [
    {
      name: 'chromium',
      use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'], channel: 'chromium' },
    },
  ],
});
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright tests in headed mode with Pytest

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-python.md

Execute Playwright tests using Pytest, explicitly opening a browser window to visually observe the test execution. The browser remains open until the tests are completed. Use the `--headed` flag to enable this mode.

```bash
pytest --headed
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright to Use Chromium New Headless Mode

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/browsers.md

These examples demonstrate how to configure Playwright to use the 'new headless mode' for Chromium by specifying the 'chromium' channel. This mode leverages the real Chrome browser, offering a more authentic and feature-rich environment for end-to-end testing compared to the traditional headless shell. Configuration varies by programming language and test runner setup.

```javascript
import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  projects: [
    {
      name: 'chromium',
      use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'], channel: 'chromium' },
    },
  ],
});
```

```java
import com.microsoft.playwright.*;

public class Example {
  public static void main(String[] args) {
    try (Playwright playwright = Playwright.create()) {
      Browser browser = playwright.chromium().launch(new BrowserType.LaunchOptions().setChannel("chromium"));
      Page page = browser.newPage();
      // ...
    }
  }
}
```

```bash
pytest test_login.py --browser-channel chromium
```

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <Playwright>
    <BrowserName>chromium</BrowserName>
    <LaunchOptions>
      <Channel>chromium</Channel>
    </LaunchOptions>
  </Playwright>
</RunSettings>
```

```bash
dotnet test -- Playwright.BrowserName=chromium Playwright.LaunchOptions.Channel=chromium
```

--------------------------------

### Debug all Playwright tests with Playwright Inspector

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-python.md

Enable the Playwright Inspector and run all tests in debug mode. This command opens a browser window and the Inspector for step-by-step debugging of your tests. The `-s` flag is often used to ensure `print` statements are captured in the terminal.

```bash
PWDEBUG=1 pytest -s
```

```batch
set PWDEBUG=1
pytest -s
```

```powershell
$env:PWDEBUG=1
pytest -s
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright Test Runner with .runsettings XML

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-csharp.md

Provides an example of a `.runsettings` XML file for configuring Playwright test runners (e.g., `Microsoft.Playwright.NUnit`, `Microsoft.Playwright.MSTest`). This configuration allows setting browser name, expect timeouts, launch options like headless mode, and environment variables for debugging selectors.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Playwright -->
  <Playwright>
    <BrowserName>chromium</BrowserName>
    <ExpectTimeout>5000</ExpectTimeout>
    <LaunchOptions>
      <Headless>true</Headless>
      <Channel>msedge</Channel>
    </LaunchOptions>
  </Playwright>
  <!-- General run configuration -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <EnvironmentVariables>
      <!-- For debugging selectors, it's recommend to set the following environment variable -->
      <DEBUG>pw:api</DEBUG>
    </EnvironmentVariables>
  </RunConfiguration>
</RunSettings>
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Tests in Headed Mode with Browser Window

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-js.md

Executes tests with visible browser windows using the --headed flag. This allows visual observation of how Playwright interacts with the website during test execution.

```bash
npx playwright test --headed
```
